checks.description: The T2 contract gates itself: trust-matrix golden is current and covered, fixture vectors are well-formed, no raw candidate secret exists in durable artifacts, gate constants match this manifest, and every -run expression below binds to at least one real test function under its build tags on the Linux target (TestManifestRunPatternsBindToRealTests) so no check can pass on zero matches.

Full adversarial fixture suite (timing.*, races.*, restore.*, redaction.*) executed via securitytest.RunConformance against a real trust engine with a spy launcher and fake clock. Skips (never passes) until T4 registers a Factory.
prerequisites: T4 backend test package calls securitytest.RunConformance with a real Factory (real trust engine, spy DescriptorLaunch, fake Clock)

checks.description: Every row of testdata/security/trust-matrix.json replayed against the real integrated trust engine (TestTrustMatrixReplayIntegrated: production control actor + SQLite trust store + hook runtime with real project directories; rows whose condition needs privileged mounts or not-yet-integrated product surface drive the real AuthorizeLaunch linearization point with the single deviated fact injected, recorded per row in the test). Complements, never replaces, the unit-level TestDecideReplaysTrustMatrix. A pass receipt must show 41 substantive row executions and zero skips.
prerequisites: Linux host for the release receipt (the replay itself also runs on the author host)

checks.description: A second local UID exercises the socket surface: connect attempt closed pre-protocol and audited, hostile runtime dir/socket objects refuse bind, diagnostics refuse foreign peers.
prerequisites: test harness with a second unprivileged UID

checks.description: Hostile frame lengths allocate nothing beyond the fixed header buffer; silent peers are disconnected at the deadline; greedy subscribers are disconnected rather than buffered unboundedly; the connection cap yields resource_exhausted.
Entire test suite under the Go race detector on the Linux CI matrix.
Architectural dependency rules hold: domain import rules, forbidden inbound edges, and the no-cgo gate.
The 13 frozen platform interfaces retain their exact shapes; drift routes through an ADR-0006 amendment, not silent edits.
Module checksums verify against go.sum for the entire pinned dependency graph.
go.mod/go.sum carry no undeclared or stale requirements.
Known-vulnerability scan over the module graph and reachable call paths; any HIGH/CRITICAL reachable finding blocks until fixed or explicitly triaged unreachable with recorded rationale.
prerequisites: golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck installed (pinned in CI by T3)

checks.description: License inventory of the full dependency graph against the allowlist in docs/security/security-readiness.md §4; a disallowed or unknown license blocks.
prerequisites: github.com/google/go-licenses installed (pinned in CI by T3)

checks.description: Secrets scan over the full working tree and complete git history with the frozen policy file; any non-allowlisted finding blocks and triggers the rotation protocol in security-readiness.md §5.
prerequisites: gitleaks >= 8.x installed (pinned in CI by T3)

checks.description: Manual red-team walk of every abuse case AB-1..AB-12 in threat-model.md §5 against the release candidate, one recorded outcome per row.
checks.command: manual: execute threat-model.md §5 rows AB-1..AB-12 against the candidate; record per-row outcome and evidence in the receipt
